body.title: [triage] Granny dispatch: check whether role_in->route_to rules are the same monolith collapse (NEXT PHASE)
body.intention: I intend to determine whether granny's dispatch rules suffer the same stack-collapse as the inference router, and if so decompose them the same way.
body.description:
NEXT PHASE — do NOT work until the inference router redesign (T-inference-* above) is complete. Akien flagged (2026-07-08) that granny's dispatch routing 'is prolly borked the same way.' Granny's match_rule consumes config rules shaped {when:{role_in:[...]}, route_to:<worker>} — a role->worker mapping that may hardcode the same way the inference triples did (worker identity baked into the rule instead of composed from a capability/availability stack).

**Affected files:** READ FIRST — unseen_university/devices/granny/daemon.py (match_rule, run_once, _select_builder, _cascade_active_workers); the granny config schema; tests/granny/.
**Design rules:** cooperative-not-hierarchical; homogeneity-over-special-case; test-plan-or-why-not.
**Scope boundary:** INVESTIGATE and report first (is it the same collapse? where?). Only propose/apply a decomposition if the investigation confirms it — file follow-up tickets rather than expanding this one.
**Test plan:** the investigation output names concretely whether granny routing hardcodes worker identity vs. composes from stacks; if a fix is warranted, it is captured as its own proven ticket(s).
**Rollback:** n/a (investigation).
**Depends on:** the inference router redesign shipping first (T-inference-migrate-consumers-cutover).
